steps.title: Use the cause in the notes
steps.content: The student's goal is to identify what causes meanders. The relevant note says a meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of a riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side.

steps.title: Avoid unrelated details
steps.content: Choice B reverses the relationship by making meanders cause high sinuosity. Choice C identifies the Cedar River but does not explain formation. Choice D explains later movement, not cause. The sentence that states erosion and redeposition form curves meets the goal. The answer is A.
